verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2, human glandular kallikrein) is a secreted serine protease produced by prostate epithelium as a pre-pro-enzyme that is processed and released into seminal fluid and serum. It has a signal peptide but no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Walking the contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool of KLK2 itself; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — prostate-restricted expression but as a secreted protease, not surface-anchored; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM-anchored isoform or cycling pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tethering to a TM partner; reversible substrate engagement only. Clinical programs (e.g., MLN2704-era and newer radioligand/TCE efforts) target PSMA, not KLK2 on surface; emerging KLK2-directed agents (e.g., JNJ-78278343) are T-cell engagers against KLK2, but KLK2 itself is secreted/pericellular — not stably surface-anchored.
predicted_key_uncertainty: JNJ-78278343 and similar KLK2-directed TCEs imply some pericellular/surface-accessible pool on prostate cells; if KLK2 is wash-resistantly retained on tumor cell surface this could shift to contextual/stable_surface_attachment.
